summary_one_line: An attribute/element namespace prefix whose bytes equal the literal string "xml", so xmlStrEqual(nameSpace, "xml") returns true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A namespace prefix passed to xmlSearchNs whose 3 bytes are exactly 0x78 0x6d 0x6c ("xml"), satisfying the string compare xmlStrEqual(nameSpace, (const xmlChar *)"xml") at tree.c:6141 and thereby taking the true branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner cmplog seeds carry literal "xml" runs in their bytes (e.g. 78 6d 6c rendered as 'xml' inside '<?xml' and in 'x.xml' substrings), and the per-role overlay shows the winner alone reaching the true-branch body (lines 6142/6161/6169 tagged [W]); the blocker counts T=12 F=6 for W vs T=0 F=53 for L confirm only W ever takes the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser naive seeds have the surrounding XML structure but never present a prefix string equal to "xml" at the compare; the blocker line for L is T=0 F=53, so naive's prefixes always mismatch and fall through to the namespace-list walk (lines 6182-6189 tagged [L]).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry recording the program's constant operand "xml" from the xmlStrEqual call, scans the input for the matching operand, and substitutes "xml" at the prefix offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must hit the exact 3-byte string by chance, which it effectively never does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is gated by a string equality against the literal "xml".
Only the winner ever takes the true side of the blocker; the loser is always false.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L6141  T=12 F=6  T=0 F=53  (xmlStrEqual(nameSpace, (const xmlChar *)"xml"))) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds contain literal "xml" byte runs.
The winner-only true-branch body is reached exclusively by the winning seed set.
The loser instead falls through to the namespace-list prefix walk.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, while naive has no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutator stack contains no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6946 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 51fd9b36079604e3` and observed the I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 3 and 4, characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on xmlStrEqual(prefix, "xml") in which cmplog shows no resolve advantage over naive (both resolve or both block at the same rate), indicating the 3-byte literal compare is not what I2S substitution unlocks.

weakest_evidence_point: The winning prefix bytes "xml" co-occur with the unrelated '<?xml' processing-instruction literal in the same seeds, so the exact_quote bytes may evidence the PI rather than the namespace-prefix operand the blocker compares.
